decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently at its upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since material tuning is maxed out, the next best high-impact action is to increase the geometric cross-section of the heat spreader. I will increase the spreader width from 0.84m to 1.0m (the maximum allowed) to maximize lateral heat spreading towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Maximizing the spreader width increases the area available for lateral heat conduction, reducing thermal resistance between the chip and the cooled side walls.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip peak temperature due to improved lateral heat spreading.
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer.
risk_notes: The spreader width is now at its absolute upper bound (1.0m), leaving no further room for geometric widening in this direction.
If the temperature constraint is still violated, subsequent steps must focus on increasing base plate conductivity or adjusting vertical positioning.
